metadata.title: 9. Dairy, Poultry, Fisheries, Forestry and other allied sectors

questions.question: Which port exports the highest value of seafood from India?
questions.explanation: Visakhapatnam Port handled $2.19 billion or 29.72% of India's seafood exports in FY24. In FY25, Visakhapatnam's seafood export value exceeded $2 billion out of India’s total $7.45 billion. The port exported 314,199 tonnes, while JNPT exported 240,253 tonnes valued at $779.49 million. Andhra Pradesh's aquaculture, mainly Vanammei shrimp, supports Visakhapatnam's status as the top exporter.

questions.question: Which industry has expanded most in India's cooperative sector recently? (UPSC Prelims 1991)
questions.explanation: As of 2024, the sugar industry is the largest in India's cooperative sector. Nearly 50% of sugar mills in India operate under cooperative societies, especially in Maharashtra. Production increased significantly in recent years due to government support, favorable policies, and ethanol blending programs. Maharashtra and Uttar Pradesh contribute the majority of cooperative sugar factories. Cooperative expansion continued through 2023-24.

questions.question: Which of the following is the highest milk producing breed of goat in India? (UPSC Prelims 1994)
questions.explanation: Jamnapari is the most popular breed for commercial goat farming in India. It is also the highest milk producing breed of goat in India It is mostly found in Uttar Pradesh.

questions.question: Which country has the highest cattle population?
questions.explanation: As of 2022, Brazil has approximately 238 million cattle according to the Brazilian Institute of Geography and Statistics. Brazil surpassed India in total cattle population. India has around 195 million cattle as per the latest FAO and national statistics. The United States cattle population is about 92 million. China has approximately 95 million cattle.

questions.question: What is animal husbandry's contribution to India's Gross Value Added?
questions.explanation: The livestock sector is a major driver of India's economy, contributing approximately 5.5% to 5.73% of the total National Gross Value Added (GVA) as of 2023-24. Within the agriculture and allied sectors, animal husbandry's contribution is significant, accounting for over 30% of the Agricultural GVA and driving rural income growth.
